verdictLine: ship — 0 crash/softlock, no fairness blockers; escalation+iPad+IUO concerns already-fixed, 4 false-positives, 1 new polish (off-theme 18s generic hint panel)

findings.title: No stuck-player escalation / idle-nudge missing
findings.note: Current code adds a one-shot idle nudge armed at revealMainUI (6s after reveal, ~11.5s from launch) that wiggles+pulses the handle, swaps the hint to 'SLIDE THE WHITE DOT TO THE RIGHT →', plays a beep/haptic, honors Reduce Motion, and is disarmed in touchesBegan on first real drag. This directly resolves the 'only ever the same looping hint' finding all five reviewers raised against the older build. No action.

findings.title: iPad boot-log stranded top-left, empty lower/right void
findings.note: Real but cosmetic-only: the anchored node is the NON-interactive boot LOG; the interactive UI (title/bar/handle/character) is correctly recentered to origin in revealMainUI (:209-213) once the camera moves to zero. On iPad the 5.5s log reads top-left with empty canvas, but nothing playable is stranded and there's no fairness/completability impact. Optional polish: center bootTextContainer.x on iPad. Lowest priority.

findings.title: Redundant completion marking (succeedLevel + markCompleted)
findings.note: Not redundant. succeedLevel→recordCompletion only writes a time stat (ProgressManager.swift:332) and does NOT insert into completedLevels; onLevelSucceeded is not overridden here so no auto-markCompleted fires. The single markCompleted(.boot) is in transitionToLevel1 (:797). One insert, no double-marking. False-positive.

findings.title: force-unwrap randomElement()! can crash
findings.note: Array literal is non-empty and immutable, so randomElement() is statically guaranteed non-nil. Cannot trap. False-positive.

findings.title: IUO progressHandle/cursorNode nil-deref risk
findings.note: Every touch path now guards `guard let progressHandle else { return }` with an explicit comment (:473-475) noting taps during the boot-log phase would otherwise deref nil; cursorNode is optional-chained everywhere (cursorNode?). The deliberately permissive early-bail PREVENTS a softlock/crash. Already-fixed / working as intended.

findings.title: handleTutorialTouchMoved swallows all moved events
findings.note: It returns true ONLY when tutorialStep == .dragToMove (guard at :785). For .tapToJump/.deviceReveal the guard fails and it returns false, so moves are not swallowed. Consuming drags during the drag-to-move tutorial step is correct. False-positive.

findings.title: Upfront 'DRAG TO 100% TO BOOT' spoils the answer / zero discovery
findings.note: Intentional and correct for a boot/onboarding screen. The jump-budget rubric doesn't apply (no platforms). Telling a first-time player exactly how to boot is good onboarding, not a fairness defect; reviewers themselves conceded it's 'acceptable for a boot scene.' The JUST KIDDING gag stays unspoiled. Working as designed.

findings.title: Inherited 18s no-progress hint panel shows off-theme generic copy
findings.codeRef: Level0_BootSequence.swift (no hintText() override) → BaseLevelScene.swift:704/:784 hintText() ?? "Try using your device's features..."
findings.note: The scene is .playing from didMove (inherits base runIntroSequence→startPlay), so the base update() no-progress nets fire here. A player still stalled at ~18s gets the base difficulty panel reading 'Try using your device's features...', which is nonsensical for a drag-the-loading-bar boot screen. The 6s idle-nudge already gave correct guidance, so impact is low, but the copy mismatch is real and was missed because reviewers saw an older build. Fix: override hintText() to return e.g. 'Drag the white dot all the way right to boot.' Polish-tier.

findings.title: Idle-nudge wiggle restore skipped if drag interrupts mid-wiggle
findings.codeRef: Level0_BootSequence.swift:271-280 wiggle ends with .run restore; :487 removeAllActions()
findings.note: touchesBegan calls progressHandle.removeAllActions(), which can cancel the wiggle before its final `.run { position.x = baseX }` restore, leaving the handle up to ~16pt off baseline. Harmless: scale is explicitly reset (:488), progress is driven by the clamped drag (monotonic, only increases), and the player's own drag immediately repositions the handle. Net 0 wiggle so an uninterrupted nudge self-corrects anyway. Cosmetic at most; optional to compute restore from a captured baseX outside the action.

findings.title: Foundation glitchTimer keeps firing while backgrounded (not invalidated on resign-active)
findings.codeRef: Level0_BootSequence.swift:301-309 startAmbientGlitches; :58 invalidate only in willMove
findings.note: glitchTimer is a repeating Foundation Timer invalidated only in willMove(from:)/deinit/completeBootSequence, not on app-background. While backgrounded it may still tick and add auto-removing flash nodes via triggerMicroGlitch. No crash, no leak (nodes self-remove, guarded by !bootComplete), purely a tiny off-screen waste. Cosmetic; would be cleaner as an SKAction (pauses with the scene) but not worth a fix on its own.

topActions: Override hintText() in BootSequenceScene to return a drag-the-loading-bar specific string (e.g. 'Drag the white dot all the way right to boot.') so the inherited 18s base no-progress panel stops showing the off-theme 'Try using your device's features...' copy.

verdictLine: ship — 0 crash/softlock survivors; 1 real fairness gap (consumed-banner recovery is non-obvious), 1 polish (whisper contrast), 1 cosmetic dead code; 4 panel findings are false-positives or already-fixed

findings.title: Header-drag has no affordance → first-timers soft-lock
findings.codeRef: LevelHeaderHUD.swift:44-117 (idlePulse breathing glow); GameRootView.swift:47-49 + Level1_Header.swift:96 (always-on dragHUD fallback)
findings.note: Current LevelHeaderHUD breathes an accent border-glow/shadow at rest (idlePulse, .onAppear) and exposes a VoiceOver accessibilityHint ('Draggable. Drag this banner down...'). configureScene calls forceHardwareFallback(for:.dragHUD), so showsFallbackOverlay is TRUE on L1 from t=0 — the cyan arrow.down.square fallback button is always present as a guaranteed path. Plus a ~22s wall-clock safety-net hint. No hard softlock. Recommendation: keep as-is.

findings.title: Drop hit-test is dangerously permissive / dilutes the 'aha'
findings.note: This is the documented CHARM FIX and is load-bearing: the accessibility/sim fallback posts a FIXED (210,240). I verified the math — on iPad the scaled pit sits at x≈[624,864], so the fixed x:210 fails the strict pit test and would route to notePlayerStruggle() → UNWINNABLE. The permissive band is exactly what makes the only no-drag path work on every canvas. Tightening it (codex/kimi suggestion) would reintroduce the iPad accessibility softlock. Do NOT tighten.

findings.title: Missed manual drop consumes the banner permanently (no re-show, non-obvious recovery)
findings.note: The HUD sets hasDropped=true (banner vanishes) for ANY drop past size.height/3, BEFORE the scene decides accept/reject. A drop that passes the HUD gate but fails the scene gate (e.g. dragged down-and-left to x<~36 on iPhone) calls notePlayerStruggle() with NO bridge AND the banner gone. hasDropped is @State on LevelHeaderHUD whose identity is keyed on levelID, so a reboot/respawn in the SAME level does NOT reset it — the banner never returns. Recovery exists only via the always-present cyan fallback button, which a player has no reason to know is now their sole path. Recommendation: only set hasDropped after the scene confirms a bridge (gate the consume on bridgeSpawned), OR snap the banner back on a scene-rejected drop.

findings.title: hintText() immediately reveals the full solution with no escalation
findings.note: Mischaracterizes current code. There IS escalation: t=0 de-spoiled whisper ('the sign with my number on it') is the intermediate hint; the explicit hintText only fires after struggleCount>=2 AND >=8s (notePlayerStruggle), or the ~22s wall-clock fallback. The single all-revealing string is correctly the FINAL tier, not the first. No change needed.

findings.title: Force-unwrapped bit/playerController would crash if make() returns nil
findings.note: BitCharacter.make() returns a non-optional BitCharacter (constructs and returns directly; never nil). The IUOs are assigned before any use within the same configureScene flow. No crash path. Cosmetic at most.

findings.title: drawCables() fully implemented but never called
findings.note: Confirmed dead code — drawCables is defined but setupBackground (and nothing else) calls it. Harmless. Recommendation: delete it, or wire it into setupBackground if the hanging cables were intended decor.

findings.title: iPhone t=0 whisper low-contrast (accent on white) and competes with spawning Bit
findings.codeRef: GlitchedNarrator.swift:101,227 (.whisper uses Colors.accent, no backing plate) + Level1_Header.swift:109-112
findings.note: Grounded: .whisper renders Colors.accent glyphs with only RGB-split ghosts and NO solid backing plate, so on L1's white foreground background it can read low-contrast and sits in the lower-center band near spawn. But this lives in shared GlitchedNarrator and affects every whisper, so it's a cross-cutting polish item, not L1-specific. Recommendation: add a faint dark backing plate to the whisper style (helps all levels).

findings.title: One-time banner wiggle/pulse when the whisper fires to draw the eye
findings.note: Largely addressed: the banner already breathes a repeating accent glow from .onAppear, so the draggable element is continuously signposted. A discrete wiggle synced to the whisper would be a marginal extra; low priority given the existing idle affordance.

findings.title: Magic numbers / dense layoutXScale-layoutYScale math should be named constants
findings.note: Maintainability only — the values are heavily commented and the invariants (gap<=130, rise<=85) are clamped at runtime (BaseLevelScene.maxJumpableGap/maxJumpableRise). I simulated the iPad serpentine across 1024x1366 / 820x1180 / 834x1194: worst forward hop is exactly 130pt gap at 83pt rise (within caps) and right→left hops overlap in X, so reachability holds. No functional bug; refactor is optional.

topActions: Gate banner consumption on scene acceptance: only set hasDropped=true once the scene confirms a bridge (or snap the banner back on a scene-rejected drop) so a near-miss drag can't strand the player with a vanished banner and only the non-obvious fallback button for recovery (Level1_Header.swift:646-671 + LevelHeaderHUD.swift:142-151).
Add a faint dark backing plate to the GlitchedNarrator .whisper style so the t=0 cyan line reads against L1's white background (GlitchedNarrator.swift ~227).
Delete the unused drawCables() (Level1_Header.swift:361-396) or wire it into setupBackground.

verdictLine: ship — 0 real fairness/crash issues; iPad void already-fixed by confined-column rewrite; 3 false-positives, 4 cosmetic survivors (dead code + iPad gust overshoot), 1 worth a quick polish

findings.title: iPad renders flat phone strip with empty void above
findings.note: Round-1 screenshots were of the OLD left-to-right + installCameraFollow build that stranded the upper band off-screen. Current code is the confined vertical zig-zag column centered on size.width/2 with NO camera-follow, gated behind isWideCanvas (h>1000 && w>600), which trips on every portrait iPad. Numeric check (834x1194, 1024x1366, 744x1133): tier spacing 80.9-82.9pt (<=85), side-to-side edge gaps 80pt (<130), finale block fits with positive left margin (80-220pt). The void is addressed in code; recommend one visual re-verification screenshot on a real iPad to close it, but no code change needed.

findings.title: Bridge collidable only above 10*visualScale -> weak breaths spawn invisible floor Bit falls through
findings.note: Visual alpha and physics are driven by the SAME visibleSegments count: when bridgeCurrentWidth<=10*vs, visibleSegments==0, so EVERY segment gets alpha=0 (index<0 never true) AND no physics body exists. There are no visible-but-non-solid planks. Above threshold, segment 0 is both the first opaque AND first solid segment (both anchored at bridge origin). No invisible-floor; coupling is correct. No action.

findings.title: Struggle path never escalates beyond generic notePlayerStruggle; soft-blow players left without targeted coaching
findings.note: Escalation exists on BOTH paths. Death -> notePlayerStruggle() -> after struggleCount>=2 && playtime>=8s -> showDifficultyHintIfNeeded -> difficultyHintDidShow() override -> showMicrophoneHint() draws the 'BLOW' mic placard. Independently, the wall-clock no-progress fallback (BaseLevelScene update: ~22s since last progress) fires the same hint even for a soft-blow player who never dies. hintText() backs it with 'Try blowing into the microphone.' The targeted coaching the reviewers asked for is already wired. No action.

findings.title: Bridge physicsBody rebuilt on every segment-count change is brittle and can drop Bit during extension
findings.note: During extension the body only GROWS (count increases, never removed), so the surface under Bit stays solid; clearGroundedIfStandingOn(bridge) keeps grounded TRUE while the new body is solid and clears it only on full retract (body nil). deltaTime is clamped to 1/30 in BaseLevelScene.update so resume spikes can't snap-collapse the span, and the asymmetric 18pt/s retract leaves seconds to cross. A body SWAP can fire didEnd(old)+didBegin(new) with a deferred 0.05s setGrounded(false), but Bit physically rests on a present solid body throughout, so any flicker is inert. Not a real drop. No action.

findings.title: Force-unwrapped optionals bit / playerController / bridge can crash
findings.note: Standard IUO-after-configureScene pattern shared by every level. updatePlaying (the only per-frame use of playerController) runs only while levelState==.playing, which is set after configureScene builds bit/playerController/bridge. No code path dereferences them pre-init. Cosmetic at most, not a crash. No action.

findings.title: iPad wind gust indicator lines overshoot the finale chasm onto/past the exit bank
findings.note: REAL: the 8 gust lines are spaced by 25*layoutXScale from absolute chasmStartX, but layoutXScale scales with width while the iPad chasm is a fixed 235pt. They extend ~68pt (mini) to ~182pt (12.9) past chasmEnd, drifting over the exit bank. These are decorative non-physics indicators, so it is purely visual — no fairness/reach impact. Quick fix: on isWideCanvas space the 8 lines across (chasmEndX-chasmStartX)/8 instead of 25*layoutXScale so they stay inside the actual finale chasm.

findings.title: Dead code: isCompactPhoneLayout (unread), drawHangingVibrationPickup (uncalled), lastMicLevel (write-only)
findings.note: REAL but cosmetic. isCompactPhoneLayout (26) is never read; drawHangingVibrationPickup (436) is never called; lastMicLevel (262) is assigned at 1035 and never read. Safe to delete all three; no behavioral effect. Cleanup only.

findings.title: Magic numbers / hardcoded iPad gate / dense file; externalize layout
findings.note: REAL maintainability nit: isWideCanvas threshold (h>1000 && w>600) and many inline column constants make this a long, dense file. Functionally correct and consistent with the other composed-iPad levels' style. Pure refactor; no player impact. Optional.

findings.title: Ambient room noise can extend the bridge before the player understands the mic mechanic
findings.note: This is by-design and net-positive: the bridge target is driven by mic power with a boosted curve, and the asymmetric slow retract means accidental ambient extension simply leaves a partial bridge that lingers — it never blocks or softlocks, and a stray loud noise that helps the player cross is harmless. The whole level's intent is 'environmental access.' Not a bug; the first successful blow even triggers the 4th-wall 'DID YOU JUST... BLOW ON YOUR PHONE?' beat that retroactively teaches the mechanic. No action.

findings.title: First encounter relies on associating 'wind' with the microphone (cold-read leap)
findings.note: Acknowledged design tension, but adequately mitigated: t=0 'LOOKS WINDY' diegetic placard + animated gusts, then a tiered escalation to an explicit 'BLOW' mic placard with sound-waves on struggle/stall, plus hintText 'Try blowing into the microphone.' This is the intended first-mic-level teaching ramp and is consistent with the de-spoil philosophy. No action.

topActions: Optional cosmetic: on iPad (isWideCanvas) space the 8 setupWindVisuals gust lines across (chasmEndX-chasmStartX)/8 from chasmStartX instead of 25*layoutXScale, so the decorative gusts stay inside the finale chasm instead of overshooting the exit bank by up to ~182pt (Level2_Wind.swift:824-843, 1085-1102).
Optional cleanup: delete dead members isCompactPhoneLayout (26-28), drawHangingVibrationPickup (436-470), and the write-only lastMicLevel (262/1035).
Verification only (no code change): capture one portrait-iPad screenshot to confirm the confined-column climb now fills the frame and the round-1 'void' is visually closed.

verdictLine: ship — 0 crash/softlocks; 1 new minor fairness edge-clip worth a 1-line fix, 1 inverse-cue polish; the panel's big iPad-void and missing-escalation findings are already-fixed or false-positives

findings.title: iPad playfield hugs the floor with a large empty TV-frame interior above (vertical void)
findings.note: Reviews were against the OLD horizontal-march layout. Current code lays the climb as a confined CENTERED zig-zag column driven by fillTierCount()+verticalTier(): on iPad Air the column spans groundY≈110→peakY≈938 (ceiling) and on 12.9" 110→1192, with the camera resting at scene-center (composedWorldWidth=0). I computed this numerically — the climb fills floor-to-ceiling and is fully visible in one frame. No void remains. No action.

findings.title: No notePlayerStruggle() override, so deaths only hit the base fallback instead of escalating level-specific guidance
findings.note: Escalation IS wired: handleDeath() calls notePlayerStruggle(); the base impl shows hintText() after struggleCount>=2 && 8s; Level3 overrides hintText() (1267) to teach BOTH halves (noise clears 1-3, silence clears the dashed 4th). DeepSeek conflated 'no override of notePlayerStruggle' with 'no escalation'. Fully functional. No action.

findings.title: isWideCanvas check (height>1000 && width>430) wrongly excludes landscape iPad from the composed layout
findings.note: Landscape iPad (e.g. 1366x1024) passes: height 1024>1000 AND width 1366>430. Only short canvases (iPad mini landscape, 744 tall) fall through to the phone layout — which is exactly what every BaseLevelScene helper does (all gate on size.height>1000), and the phone course is device-portable/byte-identical. Consistent and intentional, not a layout bug. No action.

findings.title: Rectangular laser hit bodies are not rotated to match arbitrary start/end beams (brittle)
findings.note: Every laser is authored vertical (start.x == end.x on both iPhone and composed iPad paths), so an axis-aligned rect of width 10 x beamLength is geometrically correct. Codex itself concedes 'fine for vertical beams.' Purely hypothetical future risk; no current bug. No action.

findings.title: Inverse-rule cue (dashed 4th laser + 'THIS ONE LISTENS DIFFERENTLY' placard) too subtle to read during motion
findings.note: Already hardened vs the build the panel saw: plate 156x24→224x34, font 7→10.5pt, added a deeper alpha+scale 'breath' pulse and a border shimmer, dashed beam, and the laser starts visibly OFF in silence. Cue load is acknowledged but this is now a reasonable non-spoiler t=0 teach. Residual is taste-level polish only — optionally add a reversed noise-meter glyph; not required to ship.

findings.title: iPhone half plays like a safe staircase (overlapping treads, tiny rises) rather than a laser-dodge
findings.note: By design: the lasers, not the jumps, are the gate on this level (the mechanic IS noise-shielding, not precision platforming). Rises ~25pt logical are deliberately trivial so the player focuses on the mic mechanic. Tightening platform fractions would add difficulty the level isn't trying to test. Not a defect.

findings.title: instructionPanel! force-unwrapped on addChild
findings.note: instructionPanel is assigned SKNode() on line 954, four lines above the unwrap, with no intervening nil path. The IUO can never be nil here. No crash risk. No action.

findings.title: Hardcoded 'Menlo-Bold'/'Menlo' font names instead of VisualConstants.Fonts
findings.note: The literals exactly equal VisualConstants.Fonts.main ('Menlo-Bold') / .secondary ('Menlo'), so rendering is correct — this is a token-consistency/maintainability nit only. Swap to the token if touching the file; not ship-blocking.

findings.title: TEACH laser hit-zone overlaps the spawn platform's near edge by ~5pt on the wide-spawn / narrow-next pairing
findings.codeRef: Level3_Static.swift:599-605 (composedLaserSpecs lx = midpoint of platform CENTERS), 552-553 (switchback x)
findings.note: The laser x is the midpoint of the two platform CENTERS, not the geometric center of the GAP. When a wide platform (176) sits opposite a narrow one (76), the wider deck's near edge reaches exactly columnGap/2 (25pt) from center — the same x as the laser — so the hit-zone (half-width 5*visualScale, base = deck-20) overlaps the wide deck edge by ~5pt. I reconstructed the full iPad seq and only gap(0,1) (the TEACH laser bracketing the wide tier-0 spawn) hits lx==aNear. A player standing at the spawn's jump-off edge in dead silence could graze it. MITIGATED: the player is explicitly taught to make noise, which turns this exact laser OFF before crossing, so it is an edge-clip not a guaranteed death. Fix: anchor lx to the gap geometric center ((aNear+bNear)/2) instead of (xs[a]+xs[b])/2, or hold the hit-zone a few pt off the lower deck. 1-line change.

topActions: Anchor the composed-iPad laser X to the gap's geometric center (between the platforms' NEAR edges) instead of the midpoint of their centers, so the TEACH laser hit-zone no longer overlaps the wide spawn deck's near edge by ~5pt (Level3_Static.swift:599-605).

verdictLine: ship — the headline 5-reviewer finding (isWideCanvas hardcoded false → dead iPad code) is STALE/already-fixed; iPad path is live and provably completable. Net: 0 real-unfixed of consequence, 1 minor polish, ~5 false-positives/already-fixed.

findings.title: isWideCanvas hardcoded to false → entire iPad composed-climb branch is dead code
findings.note: UNANIMOUS round-1 finding is now STALE. Current line 89 is a real computed property `size.height > 1000 && size.width > 700`, matching sibling levels. The whole buildComposedIPadLevel path is LIVE on real iPads, not dead. I numerically verified the composed climb is completable: worst-case top-to-top rise 81–84pt (≤85 safe / 91 cap) and every adjacent edge-to-edge gap 7–108pt (≤130 safe), wide pads overlap as reachable step-ups. The 'delete or re-enable the dead branch' recommendation is moot — it's the active, correct iPad layout. No action.

findings.title: Idle-but-alive stuck player never earns the volume-button hint (escalation only on death)
findings.note: Addressed by the base-class NO-PROGRESS HINT SAFETY-NET. `lastProgressAt` is seeded at startPlay() (BaseLevelScene:490) and, if no forward progress for ~22s (×1.75 with extendedHintTimers), update() calls showDifficultyHintIfNeeded() unconditionally — independent of notePlayerStruggle()/death. So a passive player standing still still gets hintText() ('Press your device's Volume-Down button…'). The exact gap the panel flagged is closed. No action.

findings.title: hintText() is a full reveal / no graduated 'too loud' vs 'water rising' ladder
findings.note: Genuinely present: hintText() returns one static string naming Volume-Down outright, and notePlayerStruggle() only escalates that single string. The atmospheric t=0 panel + wolf sleep-talk provide softer cues first, and the hint only fires after struggle/idle, so it isn't an immediate spoil. A context-sensitive ladder (vague quiet cue → water cue → explicit button) would be nicer but is pure polish, not fairness — the player has earned the reveal by the time it shows. Optional: keep current single hint; low priority.

findings.title: Sleep-talk line 'lower the volume… please' spoils the mechanic before experimentation
findings.note: It's one of SIX randomized lines (sleepTalkLines), cycled via sleepTalkIndex starting at 0 with a 4s interval, and only while the wolf is sleeping. The explicit 'lower the volume' line is index 5 — it appears ~24s in at the earliest, after several atmospheric lines, by which point experimentation is expected. It's an intentional diegetic 4th-wall nudge, not an immediate tell. Not a bug.

findings.title: KVO observer updates creature/indicator but skips updateWaterLevel(); relies on InputEventBus round-trip
findings.note: The bus subscription (BaseLevelScene:682-688) is wired in subscribeToEvents() during didMove, BEFORE setupVolumeObserver() ever installs the KVO. So when KVO posts .volumeChanged it always reaches handleGameInput() → updateWaterLevel(). The 'bus fires before scene is delegate' race cannot occur (KVO is installed strictly after subscription). Only true edge: while paused, handleGameInput is gated on .playing so water won't update though the KVO-direct creature update would — but that's a paused-state cosmetic, self-corrects on resume/next change. Not player-facing.

findings.title: No-hardware fallback seeds currentVolume=0.15, making the hazard 'nearly solved'
findings.note: This branch only runs when usesHardware(.volume)==false, which on real devices is the accessibility/Switch-Control fallback or the simulator (forceHardwareFallback at :196). On a real device with the hardware rocker, usesHardware is true and the KVO observer is live. Seeding 0.15 (safe/asleep) so the level is passable WITHOUT the unavailable hardware input is the correct accessibility/sim behavior, not a shipping fairness leak. Intentional.

findings.title: waterHazardActive only true >0.85 while comment says 'Flood!' at >0.7 — player stands in deep water 0.7–0.85 without drowning
findings.note: Intentional grace band, not a bug. The flood visibly rises across 0.7–0.85 (visual tension) but only becomes lethal above 0.85, giving the player a margin to react before drowning. This is forgiving design, the opposite of unfair. The drown check (:329-334) correctly compares Bit.position.y to the water top. Verified the iPad flood reaches the spawn floor at peak volume but not the upper tiers — climbing + staying quiet is the intended safe path.

findings.title: playerController is an implicitly-unwrapped optional
findings.note: It is assigned in setupBit() during configureScene() before any update()/touch can run, and every use (updatePlaying, touch handlers, handleDeath) occurs after setup. Matches the sibling-level pattern. No crash path. Stylistic only.

topActions: (Optional, polish) Replace the single static hintText() reveal with a 2–3 step context-sensitive ladder driven by notePlayerStruggle()/water state (vague quiet cue → 'the water is rising' cue → explicit Volume-Down), so the button name is the last rung rather than the first hint. Low priority — current behavior is already fair because the reveal is gated behind struggle/idle.

verdictLine: ship-with-one-fix — 1 new iPad softlock (charging-at-launch), 5 false-positives, 3 already-fixed by the consensus commit, 1 minor polish

findings.title: iPad softlock when device is already charging at level launch
findings.note: On iPad the spawn is relocated OFF the central boarding platform to the bottom-left climb base (x=130). But if UIDevice is already charging/full at configureScene, the plug auto-triggers at t=0.8s and rides the elevator to the top EMPTY (Bit is on the left climb, plugContactCount==0). After arrival, triggerPlugAnimation is hard-gated by hasPlugArrived, so replug can't summon it again. The iPad climb deliberately cannot reach the exit on its own (bridge >maxRise below exit, peak >maxGap away) — it only deposits Bit on the boarding platform. With the plug parked unreachably at the top, a player who climbs without dying is stranded forever. (Dying accidentally rescues them, since post-arrival spawnPoint is moved onto the exit platform — but that requires a death.) iPhone is immune because it spawns ON the boarding platform and rides the plug up. FIX: on the isWideCanvas path, defer the already-charging auto-trigger until Bit reaches/contacts the central start platform, or relocate the iPad spawn onto the boarding platform, or let .deviceCharging re-arm the plug when Bit is at the boarding point and hasn't yet ridden.

findings.title: "PLUG IN YOUR CHARGER" t=0 spoiler label gives the trick away on entry
findings.note: The de-spoil commit removed the permanent label. configureScene now shows only the atmospheric 'I'M... FADING. SO COLD. FEED ME.' tease; the explicit 'plug in your charger' solution lives solely in the EARNED hintText(). The pulsing empty-battery icon is the only t=0 visual nudge. All five reviewers read the older build. No action.

findings.title: handleDeath does not call notePlayerStruggle, so falls don't escalate the hint
findings.note: handleDeath() now calls notePlayerStruggle() on every death (line 1166), which fires showDifficultyHintIfNeeded after struggleCount>=2 && elapsedPlayTime>=8s (BaseLevelScene:744-749). Combined with the inherited ~22s no-progress fallback, a stuck player is escalated. Reviewed against the older build.

findings.title: deltaTime spike on background-resume drops the sinking plug out of bounds
findings.note: BaseLevelScene.update() already clamps clampedDt = min(dt, 1.0/30.0) and passes that into updatePlaying(deltaTime:). L5's sink math uses the supplied (clamped) deltaTime, AND the sink is independently floor-clamped to plugPlatformBaseY - plugMaxSink (90pt), which stays far above the death zone. No spike can drop the platform. Not a bug.

findings.title: handleExit double-transition: succeedLevel() then a self-scheduled transitionToNextLevel()
findings.note: succeedLevel() does NOT call transitionToNextLevel() — it only sets state .succeeded and runs onLevelSucceeded()/victory. L5 explicitly drives its own transition after the fadeOut. transitionToNextLevel is itself guarded by `!= .transitioning`, and succeedLevel by `== .playing`, so even a double exit-contact can't double-fire. Intended pattern.

findings.title: buildIPadClimb silently returns when padTierCount < 4, stranding wide screens with no exit
findings.note: isWideCanvas requires height>1000 && width>430; the app is portrait-locked (project.yml:43-44), so this only hits real iPads (width>=700). With max iPad insets ~24/20, padPlayableBandHeight = height-264 >= ~760, giving padTierCount = ceil(760/78)+1 ~= 11 — always well above 4. The guard is purely defensive and unreachable on real hardware. (Theoretical only: if it ever did trip, setupBit would still spawn on an unbuilt tier-0 — but it can't trip.)

findings.title: Carry deltaY breaks under a parent/worldNode transform; lastTrackedPlugY set too early
findings.note: No worldNode/parent transform exists in L5 — giantPlug and bit are both direct scene children in scene coordinates, and no installCameraFollow is called so the camera never pans node space. lastTrackedPlugY is re-synced EVERY frame (line 1093) before the isRidingPlug check, so when contact first begins there is no accumulated jump; the initial set at line 926 is harmless. Carry is correct.

findings.title: Magic wide-canvas constant (size.width > 430) instead of a form-factor helper
findings.note: Functionally correct for every portrait device (iPhones <=430 wide, iPads >700), and the app is portrait-locked so landscape can't sneak an iPhone past it. designWidth=430 is just the iPhone max width. At most a readability nit, not a defect — no behavioral change warranted.

findings.title: didEnd unconditionally schedules setGrounded(false) even if another ground body still touches
findings.note: didEnd clears grounded after 0.05s with no 'still-touching' guard, so stepping off one platform while resting on another briefly marks Bit airborne. In L5 the practical impact is negligible: climb platforms are spaced so Bit isn't straddling two ground bodies, the 0.16s coyote window (PlayerController:26) covers the 0.05s false-airborne gap for jumping, and the plug->exit dismount ends the level on contact. Shared base-pattern nit; fix by only clearing grounded when no ground contact remains, but low priority for L5.

findings.title: Force-removed implicitly-unwrapped `floor`; dead `_ = startPlatformTopY`
findings.note: `floor` is assigned in buildShaft (always run in configureScene) before breakFloor() is reachable, so it's never nil; removeFromParent() on an already-removed node is a harmless no-op even if called twice. `_ = startPlatformTopY` is an intentional unused-parameter silencer with an explanatory comment. Neither is a defect.

topActions: Fix the iPad already-charging-at-launch softlock: gate the t=0 auto-trigger behind Bit reaching the boarding platform on isWideCanvas (or spawn iPad Bit on the boarding platform / let .deviceCharging re-arm the plug pre-ride). This is the only player-impacting survivor.

verdictLine: ship — 2 real polish/fairness survivors (stuck instruction panel + hazards live on load), 3 false-positives (force-unwraps, flat-hintText, over-large max-brightness hitboxes), 1 cosmetic (iPhone t=0 caption), jump budget and grounded-resync verified clean

findings.title: Instruction panel never auto-dismisses if device enters above 0.6 brightness
findings.codeRef: Level6_Brightness.swift:1668-1679 (dismiss guard) + :101 (initial read) + BrightnessManager.swift:24-26 (activate re-posts current brightness)
findings.note: Confirmed real. configureScene sets currentBrightness = UIScreen.main.brightness (l101), then BrightnessManager.activate() async-posts brightnessChanged(currentDeviceBrightness). In handleGameInput the dismiss/notePlayerProgress fires only when `level > 0.6 && oldBrightness <= 0.6`; if the device starts >0.6 then oldBrightness is already >0.6 so the guard is always false — the BRIGHTNESS tooltip persists forever and notePlayerProgress is never called. Not a softlock (platforms are already solid, level fully completable; the base wall-clock hint fallback still works). Kimi was right. Fix: on load, if the initial brightness already clears the ghost threshold, fade the panel and call notePlayerProgress().

findings.title: Sun/burn hazard active on load if device starts at/above 0.95 brightness, before any teach beat
findings.codeRef: Level6_Brightness.swift:101-106 (configureScene seeds currentBrightness then updateBurnZones/updateMaxBrightnessSun) + :531-555 (sun hazard enable) + :344-376 (burn zones enable)
findings.note: Real but mitigated. Entering at >=0.95 immediately enables the sun hazard body and burn-zone hazards. However the spawn is at the bottom of the climb and ALL hazards are clustered at the TOP (burn zones over the top platforms; sun over the finale summit), so there is no spawn-kill or first-jump death — the player must climb several platforms before reaching the kill band, and updateBrightnessCommentary() at l106 fires the 'MY EYES! THE GOGGLES DO NOTHING!' alert on load as a verbal warning. So a teach signal exists even when entering hot. Codex/kimi flagged correctly. Optional fix: keep hazard bitmasks disabled until at least one UV platform has solidified once (or until the player has lowered then re-raised brightness), giving a clean first solidify before the ceiling can kill.

findings.title: Force-unwraps on maxBrightnessSun!/screenFlash!/instructionPanel!/burnWarning!/sunIcon!/brightnessBar!/brightnessIndicator!
findings.note: False-positive for crash purposes. Every one of these `!` unwraps occurs on the same or immediately-following line after the property is assigned a non-optional SKNode/SKShapeNode initializer (e.g. screenFlash = SKShapeNode(...) then addChild(screenFlash!)). The value is provably non-nil at the unwrap site; none are read across an async/optional boundary. configureScene runs exactly once (BaseLevelScene hasConfigured guard at :244), so no re-entrant nil window. Style nit at most, not a runtime crash risk.

findings.title: hintText() returns a single flat string, so struggling players get no escalating guidance
findings.note: False-positive. Escalation lives in the TIMING pipeline, not in text variation. notePlayerStruggle() (called on every death, l1757) shows the hint after 2 deaths + 8s; a no-progress timer and a ~22s wall-clock fallback also fire it for a stalled player who never dies. A single canonical hint string is the intended design across all 33 levels; the escalation curve is in BaseLevelScene, which the scene correctly drives via notePlayerProgress() / notePlayerStruggle(). The mechanism is sound.

findings.title: Sun hazard 280x50 rect and burn-zone radius 40 (vs 35 visual) are larger than their drawn art
findings.note: Largely false-positive on fairness. These hazards are only ACTIVE at >=0.95 — the explicitly-punished max-brightness state the player is meant to avoid entirely. The whole mechanic is 'do not sit at max,' so a kill band that is slightly wider than its rays/burst is consistent with broadly punishing max brightness rather than an unfair pixel-precise hitbox the player must thread. Bit collides with `ground` only (BitCharacter:82), so hazards are contact-only triggers with no physical push. Could tighten the rect to match the ray span as a polish pass, but it does not produce an unfair death in normal play.

findings.title: iPhone t=0 opening tease caption can sit on/near the top of the climb column
findings.note: Minor and cosmetic. The compact tease band bottom is layoutTopY-168 while the climb's usableTop is layoutTopY-170 — only ~2pt of clearance, so on some renders the transient 2.8s caption can crowd the topmost platform/exit. The wrap + backing-plate logic is otherwise correct. Not a fairness or completability issue (auto-fades in <3s). If touched, lift bandBottom a few pt or shrink fontSize to guarantee separation from the climb top.

findings.title: Grounded-state resync when a platform de-solidifies under Bit (and contact-end handling)
findings.note: Verified correct, no bug — noting it because it is the runtime/state-edge class the brief asked to scrutinize. When brightness drops and a platform under Bit loses its ground bitmask, updateSinglePlatform clears currentGroundPlatform and calls setGrounded(false); the inverse re-grounds via isBitResting(). currentGroundPlatform is a single weak ref (no contact COUNTER, so no underflow), and didEnd only clears it when the ending contact IS the current ground node, so landing on a new platform mid-transition does not spuriously un-ground Bit. Base update() clamps deltaTime to 1/30 and foreground-return re-posts brightness via configure(), so resume re-syncs cleanly. No softlock.

topActions: On scene load, compare the initial UIScreen.main.brightness against the ghost/0.6 threshold: if already past it, fade out the instruction panel and call notePlayerProgress() so returning/high-brightness players are not stuck with a permanent BRIGHTNESS tooltip (kimi's panel-stuck bug).

Gate the sun + burn-zone hazard bitmasks so they stay disabled until at least one UV platform has solidified once (or the player has actively raised brightness), so a device that starts at 100% gets one clean teach/solidify beat before the burn ceiling can kill (codex/kimi).

verdictLine: ship — 0 real fixes; the headline panel findings are already-fixed, the freeze-floor concern is already-fixed (1.5s, not 1.0s), and the two code-bug claims are false-positives. Only soft polish (iPad wayfinding, stale comments, single-shot hint) remains.

findings.title: Duplicate panel both opening with "SOME MOMENTS REFUSE TO MOVE..." (discovery + instruction)
findings.codeRef: Level7_Screenshot.swift:125 showDiscoveryPanel; grep shows no showInstructionPanel in this file
findings.note: Current L7 has exactly ONE panel (showDiscoveryPanel, line 116/125-159) and no showInstructionPanel — grep across the file confirms only one occurrence of the string at line 152. Claude even quotes a different line ("THE BRIDGE ONLY EXISTS WHEN NOTHING IS LOOKING") proving they read an older build. No action.

findings.title: First-encounter unfair: instruction text doesn't tell players HOW to freeze the bridge
findings.note: Intentional discovery-first design: t=0 line is a deliberate non-spoiler tease; hintText() (line 1252) explicitly spells out "press the Side button + Volume Up" and is surfaced by the base class after 2 deaths (struggleCount>=2 && >=8s, BaseLevelScene:747) or a 22s wall-clock fallback. The reveal arrives; not a softlock. No action.

findings.title: Shortest degraded freeze is only ~1.0s + grace, too tight for the 220pt chasm
findings.note: The floor is now clamped to 1.5s (case default -> 1.5), and the comment at 962-968 documents the raise from 1.0->1.5 for exactly this reason. At moveSpeed 245 the 220pt cross is ~0.90s, leaving ~0.6s margin. Codex's literal suggested fix ("keep minimum freeze at 1.5s") is already implemented. The "1.0s" mentions remaining at lines 1052/1122 are stale comment text only.

findings.title: Re-screenshotting while frozen resets frozenTimeRemaining but not the warning flicker, causing false blink
findings.note: freezeBridge() returns early via `guard !isBridgeFrozen else { return }` (line 993), and handleGameInput is also gated by a 2.0s cooldown (line 1157). A second screenshot while frozen is a no-op — frozenTimeRemaining is never reset, so the described mis-blink cannot occur. The warning flicker is a per-frame alpha computed inline (no persistent SKAction to leak), and freezeBridge sets segment.alpha=1.0 on solidify anyway. No action.

findings.title: bridgeGrace drops the bridge while Bit is mid-air during the grace jump → unfair death
findings.note: dropBridge() only clears the segments' categoryBitMask (removes collision). If Bit jumped off during the 0.2s grace he has already left the surface, so removing collision from a platform he's no longer touching does not kill him — death comes only from the y=-50 death zone. This is the coyote mechanic working as intended (the grace exists precisely to let him jump off). No action.

findings.title: iPad: finale chasm/exit scroll off-screen at spawn; upper-center canvas reads dead-white
findings.note: True but minor: this is a horizontally-scrolling course (camera-follow clamps to courseExtent, exit IS reachable on-screen as the player climbs) — analogous to any side-scroller where the goal isn't visible at spawn. The exit arrow + staircase guide direction. Optional polish: seed a faint ghost-preview of the finale bridge or a directional cue so the mid-canvas isn't empty at t=0. Not a fairness/completability issue.

findings.title: Hints don't escalate gradually — single full-answer reveal rather than soft-nudge-then-reveal
findings.note: Accurate as a polish note: handleDeath only calls notePlayerStruggle() and the reveal is one-shot (full button combo at 2 deaths / 8s). gemini/kimi's "waits 18s" is overstated — the struggle path fires at the 8s floor after 2 deaths, not 18s. Optional nicety: override to show a soft "a screenshot pins a moment" after death 1 and the explicit combo only after death 3. Low impact; current behavior is already fair.

findings.title: Unused designWidth constant
findings.note: Confirmed dead — declared and never referenced. Harmless. Delete on next cleanup pass.

findings.title: bridgeSpanHalfWidth() adds a magic +30 to a center-derived half width
findings.note: Intentional, not a bug: the half-width is computed from segment CENTERS (first/last), so it under-measures by ~half a segment per side; the +30 pads the on-bridge hit-test outward so the coyote-grace "is the player standing on the bridge" check is permissive at the bridge edges. A permissive grace test that prevents an unfair edge-drop is correct by design. No action.

findings.title: Global screenshot gesture burns screenshotCount during the iPad climb before the finale
findings.codeRef: Level7_Screenshot.swift:992-999 freezeBridge increments screenshotCount unconditionally; 1154-1164 handleGameInput
findings.note: On the iPad climb the screenshot gesture is global, so a player who screenshots early (off-screen from the finale bridge) still increments screenshotCount and triggers the flash/haptic/timer. By the finale the freeze can already be at the 1.5s floor. Not a softlock (1.5s still > 0.9s cross) and screenshotCount resets on death; arguably intended (the OS gesture is global). Could optionally only escalate when the bridge is near-viewport. Very low impact.

topActions: (optional polish) iPad wayfinding: seed a faint ghost-preview of the finale bridge or a directional cue so the upper-center canvas isn't dead-white at spawn and the player senses where the climb leads
(optional polish) graduate the hint: soft "a screenshot pins a moment" nudge after death 1, explicit Side+Vol-Up combo after death 3, instead of one-shot full reveal
(cosmetic) delete the unused designWidth constant and scrub the stale "1.0s freeze" comments at lines 1052/1122/968 to match the actual 1.5s floor

verdictLine: ship — 0 softlocks; iPad relentless near-max diagonals (polish) is the only survivor worth touching; 4 false-positives (shadow-enemy off-platform, hint-no-escalation, fallback desync, trapped-in-platform), 1 latent robustness nit, discoverability already-fixed

findings.title: Invisible core action / discoverability of the OS dark-mode switch
findings.note: The 'players won't realize the toggle is the system switch' concern is now addressed by a 3-stage escalation: t=0 atmospheric clue panel, a one-time soft GlitchedNarrator nudge at 7s ('THAT MOON ISN'T DECOR. FLIP YOUR DEVICE'S DARK MODE...'), and the full hintText reveal after struggle. Claude's own suggestion was implemented. No action.

findings.title: iPad climb pins every beat near the combined jump ceiling (85pt gap + ~82pt rise simultaneously)
findings.note: Verified numerically: iPad gap is exactly 85pt edge-to-edge (pitch 200 − halfW 65 − halfW 50) and tierStep is ~78–83pt on every beat. Each is individually within budget (gap«130, rise<85 safe), and the jump IS completable — Bit arrives over platform B's top at y≈88 (>82 surface) while descending, ~6pt vertical clearance. But it is a near-max DIAGONAL on literally every beat with no breathing room except the one rest pair, so it feels relentless and unforgiving on iPad. Codex was directionally right; his 'exact ceiling' wording overstated (tierStep is under 85, gap is well under 130). Recommend widening solid/dual platforms by ~15–20pt or dropping pitch to ~185 to buy margin on the diagonals. Not a softlock.

findings.title: iPhone shadow enemy patrols off the left edge of the light platform
findings.note: Codex mis-modeled the patrol. Enemy starts at center −20, then moveBy +40 (to +20) THEN moveBy −40 (back to −20) — it never goes left of −20. With a 14pt hazard radius the leftmost hazard edge is −34, while the 80pt platform's left edge is −40, so the hazard stays fully on the platform footprint. The hazard is also only active in dark mode, the intended 'lingering here is a hazard' semantic. No bug.

findings.title: hintText() never escalates — same full-spoiler string every time
findings.note: Misreads the design. Escalation is in the GATE (whether any hint shows), not the text. The hint is suppressed until struggleCount>=2 AND >=8s elapsed; before that the player gets only the t=0 atmospheric panel and the 7s soft nudge. The single full hintText is the deliberate final tier. That is a correctly graduated 3-stage reveal, not a missing escalation.

findings.title: Fallback toggle desyncs game state from real system appearance
findings.note: The in-scene fallback toggle is created ONLY when needsFallbackUI is true — i.e. when the system appearance CANNOT be changed (MDM/hardware-free/sim). In that mode there is no live system trait to diverge from; the toggle is the sole input. If the real system trait later changes, .darkModeChanged → applyAppearance re-syncs and wins. No softlock, no player-facing consequence.

findings.title: Player can be permanently trapped if a dual platform solidifies around Bit's bounding box
findings.note: 'Permanently trapped' is overstated. SpriteKit's penetration resolver ejects an overlapping dynamic body over a few frames rather than freezing it, and even in the worst case the full-width death zone at y:−50 catches any fall and respawnAt:spawnPoint restores a clean grounded state. So the worst realistic outcome is a brief jitter or a recoverable death, never a softlock. (Also requires the rare timing of flipping mode while mid-passing a ghost platform.) Not worth a guard.

findings.title: forceDarkMode UserDefaults could be left in the wrong global state on abnormal exit
findings.note: Already hardened: forceDarkMode is restored to true in BOTH onLevelSucceeded (before the 0.5s fade) and willMove(from:). The only uncovered path is a hard crash, after which the next launch re-runs configureScene which re-sets it false then the delayed read corrects — self-healing. Codex's 'scoped lifecycle helper' is a nice-to-have, not a bug.

findings.title: iPhone rest->door bypass-block depends on step hitting its 50pt cap
findings.note: The 3.55 multiplier closes the rest->door skip (107.5pt > 91 apex) ONLY when step==50, which needs riseBand>=500. Verified every real portrait iPhone (SE 667 → riseBand 527, up through Pro Max) clamps step to 50, so the bypass stays blocked TODAY, and the app is portrait-locked (Info.plist single UIInterfaceOrientationPortrait). But the guard is implicit: if a future smaller canvas / split-view / orientation drop ever pushes riseBand below ~500, step shrinks and rest->door silently falls under the apex (86.5pt at step=40), re-opening the very dark->light flip-skip this commit was meant to prevent. Recommend computing the door multiplier from a fixed apex margin rather than assuming step==50, or asserting step==50 on phone. Latent only — not reachable on shipping devices.

findings.title: Cluster (REST-companion) ledge uses alwaysW(130) though comment calls it a 'wide ledge'
findings.note: Minor: the rest beat is a same-tier pair — cluster(130) + rest(170). Both exist and the flat hop between them is a trivial 50pt gap, so gameplay is unaffected; it only slightly under-reads as the intended 'wide breath' pause. Optional: bump cluster to restW for visual symmetry. Cosmetic.

findings.title: Discoverability nudge accumulator immune to resume deltaTime spike
findings.note: Checked the resume-spike class: BaseLevelScene clamps dt to 1/30 (line 698) before passing clampedDt into updatePlaying (line 720), and the nudge timer accumulates that clamped value. A backgrounding spike cannot prematurely fire the 7s nudge. Safe.

topActions: iPad only: relax the relentless near-max diagonal — widen solid/dual platforms ~15–20pt or drop pitch to ~185 so every beat isn't an 85pt gap + ~82pt rise with ~6pt landing clearance (Level8_DarkMode.swift:630-638).

Harden the iPhone rest->door bypass-block so it doesn't silently depend on step==50: derive the door multiplier from a fixed apex margin (or assert step==50 on phone) so a future smaller/split canvas can't re-open the flip-skip (Level8_DarkMode.swift:439/526).
Optional cosmetic: give the .cluster rest-companion ledge restW(170) so the 'wide breath' pause reads as intended (Level8_DarkMode.swift:703).

verdictLine: ship — 0 real fixes worth doing; 1 new low-pri fairness nit (regrow-under-feet), most panel findings already-fixed or intentional-by-design

findings.title: No early/escalating hint; notePlayerStruggle never wired; stuck player waits ~22s
findings.note: Fixed in current build: tickPreHint() fires a non-spoiler nudge ('THE TREE ISN'T DONE GROWING. IT NEEDS TIME.') at preHintDelay=13s of zero-growth play, and handleDeath() now calls notePlayerStruggle() (base escalates at struggleCount>=2 & >=8s). The 22s wall-clock fallback (lastProgressAt seeded at play start) also exists. Round-1 reviews were against the older build. No action.

findings.title: t=0 panel 'ALL THINGS TAKE THEIR TIME...' too vague; first encounter reads as 'wait in place'
findings.note: Intentional: deliberately non-spoiler t=0 line matching the L11+ convention; the explicit 'Leave the app for a while' clue is earned at hintText() and now backstopped by the 13s pre-hint + death escalation. The vagueness is design, not a defect. Leave as-is.

findings.title: composedWorldWidth math: courseLeft = leftMargin - 80 - leftMargin (= -80) contradicts comment
findings.note: Real but benign: max(0, -80)=0 so the world spans x=0..courseRight, FULLY including the left scenery margin and base pad (wider, not narrower). The 'intended' value (70) would actually CHOP 70pt off the left. Runtime behavior is correct; only the comment/expression is misleading. Optional cleanup: 'let courseLeft = leftMargin - 80' + keep max(0,..). No player impact.

findings.title: Tree-stage threshold logic duplicated verbatim in applyTimePassage and handleGameInput
findings.note: Real maintainability nit (the .timePassageSimulated debug path mirrors applyTimePassage's 5/15/30s ladder). Extract a single stageFor(secondsAway:gameYears:) helper. No gameplay effect; not worth a risky change pre-ship.

findings.title: Force-unwraps of freshly-created optionals (signNode!, clockDisplay!, instructionPanel!, saplingNode!, fullTreeNode!)
findings.note: Each optional is assigned then unwrapped on adjacent lines within the same builder, with no intervening mutation — cannot be nil. treeContainer is set in buildClusterStructures() (called from buildLevel) before createSapling() runs. No crash risk.

findings.title: isWideCanvas uses magic-number threshold (size.height>1000 && size.width>390)
findings.note: Documented and consistent with the shared Phase-0 helpers (playableGroundY/verticalTier all gate on size.height>1000). Not fragile in practice. No action.

findings.title: iPad misjump on the long staircase sends Bit back to the base pad
findings.note: Verified reachable: tierCount=10 clamps per-tier rise to exactly 85 (band/(9)=120>85), pitch=122<=130, so every hop is within budget. Computed launch groundY=875, cliffHeight=280, exit=1185 (below ceiling 1192 and title cap 1252); top branch surface-top 1184 sits 29pt above the cliff top and its right edge meets the cliff left edge. Falling = respawn at spawnPoint on the launch floor — standard platformer cost, not a softlock or unfairness.

findings.title: Backgrounding to upgrade the tree while standing on a lower branch drops Bit (foothold removed mid-stand)
findings.note: growTreeToStage removes the old fullTreeNode (current branch) and rebuilds the next tier collision-DISABLED for a 1.5s grow, after a 1.5s SYNCING overlay. If the player climbed the young/mature ladder then backgrounds again to advance, the foothold vanishes and Bit falls to the death zone and respawns at spawnPoint. Not a softlock (respawn lands on the launch floor and the now-grown tree is fully climbable) and uncommon, but it silently erases climb progress. Low priority; if touched, snap Bit to spawnPoint or briefly suspend the death zone during a re-grow.

findings.title: groundContacts contact-counter underflow / desync on death-respawn
findings.note: Examined the contact-counter class explicitly: didEnd uses max(0, groundContacts-1) so no underflow, and the 0.05s debounce re-checks count==0 at fire time. playBufferDeath toggles isDynamic false->true (ends then re-begins contacts) and PlayerController.cancel() zeroes activeTouchCount. No stuck-touch or counter desync crash. The deltaTime-spike-on-resume class is also benign: appBackgrounded deltaTime is real wall-clock time fed only into gameYears/stage selection (not physics), and update() separately clamps physics dt to 1/30s.

verdictLine: ship — 0 real fixes needed; 5 false-positives/already-fixed, 1 new polish-only item. Level is completable and fair on both device classes.

findings.title: Explicit hint gated 18-22s behind a 5-7s crusher death (hint pacing too slow)
findings.codeRef: BaseLevelScene.swift:744-750 notePlayerStruggle; Level9_Orientation.swift:1731 handleDeath calls notePlayerStruggle
findings.note: WRONG against current code. handleDeath() calls notePlayerStruggle() every death, and BaseLevelScene now escalates: struggleCount>=2 && elapsedPlayTime>=8s fires showDifficultyHintIfNeeded() immediately — independent of the 18s no-progress timer. So the explicit hintText() ('Rotate the device to landscape...') surfaces on the 2nd death (~8-12s), not after 18-22s. The reviewers saw the older single-fallback build. No action.

findings.title: groundContacts can underflow to 0 while Bit still rests on the floor (false-airborne)
findings.note: The refcount is correct. didBegin increments per ground body, didEnd decrements (clamped >=0), and the 0.05s deferred check only sets airborne when groundContacts<=0. Leaving a corridor wall while on the floor takes the count 2->1, NOT to 0 — Bit stays grounded. handleDeath resets to 0 then respawn-completion sets it to 1, preventing drift. A Set<SKNode> would be marginally more robust against unbalanced begin/end but the integer is sound here. No bug.

findings.title: updateWorldScale animates worldNode xScale/yScale over 0.5s; physics-body scaling can trap/erratically collide the player during rotation
findings.note: Overstated. The animated scale only runs on rotation. Rotating portrait->landscape DISARMS the crusher (isCrusherActive=false) and WIDENS the gap (18->100), so no hazard is live and the walls move APART — Bit cannot be pinched or killed. The only live bodies are static ground/floor/exit; a 0.5s ease-scale of a static floor under a resting dynamic body is benign. The narrowing case (landscape->portrait) is a player-initiated regression and SpriteKit pushes the dynamic body out rather than trapping it. Not a softlock.

findings.title: updateCorridorPhysics dispatched async after 0.5s while walls animate — half-second visual/collision desync; non-animated path also starts with stale bodies
findings.codeRef: Level9_Orientation.swift:1482-1498 wall .moveTo + asyncAfter updateCorridorPhysics; 1124 createCorridor calls it synchronously
findings.note: Mostly wrong. (a) 'stale at start' is false: createCorridor() calls updateCorridorPhysics() SYNCHRONOUSLY at build, so bodies exist at gap=18 before the +0s async re-run. (b) The animation desync window does not overlap player presence: the wall physics bodies are attached to the wall nodes and MOVE with the .moveTo animation (body size is constant 350x25), and Bit must walk ~170pt from spawn (170) to the corridor (~340-690 local) which takes far longer than the 0.5s settle. Calling it synchronously would be a tidy refactor but there is no reachable fairness break. Polish-only, optional.

findings.title: Demo-closer solution lives outside the touchscreen — a player who never rotates can dead-end at the paywall
findings.note: Mitigated in current code, so not a true dead-end. There is a looping 90-degree phone-rotation icon + circular arrow in the instruction panel, the t=0 discovery tease, AND an EXPLICIT escalating hint ('Rotate the device to landscape — turn the phone sideways and the corridor widens') that fires after the 2nd death (~8-12s) via notePlayerStruggle, plus the AccessibilityManager registers 'Rotate to landscape'. A stuck player is told the answer in plain words quickly. Real concern is only discoverability polish, not completability.

findings.title: iPad portrait wastes canvas — flat course marooned lower-left, upper/right read as empty grid; iPhone clips crusher/skull off left edge
findings.codeRef: Level9_Orientation.swift:137-198 cacheIPadFramingIfNeeded lift; 367-491 drawIPadRightShaft; 300-302 phone worldNode pin
findings.note: Genuine but cosmetic. The current code already added a substantial lift (walk line targeted ~0.44 of band) plus drawIPadHazardFraming/drawIPadRightShaft to dress the exposed bands, so it is much improved from the reviewed build, but the framing is still a centered/lower-weighted strip rather than truly edge-to-edge, and on iPhone the crusher (the motivator to flee right) starts partly off the left edge by design (crusher local x=70, left edge of 150-wide body at -5 screen-ish under fit scale). Lowest-priority visual polish; mechanic and reachability are unaffected. Optional.

findings.title: Double-nested DispatchQueue.main.async when posting orientationChanged
findings.note: Harmless code smell: the orientation post is wrapped in two nested DispatchQueue.main.async, deferring the event by two extra runloop hops. No functional impact (the event still fires, mechanic works, faceUp/faceDown/unknown are correctly filtered and an initial state is sent). Collapse to a single async for clarity. Not worth a dedicated change unless touching the file.

topActions: (Optional, polish) Tighten iPad framing so the flat course centers/fills edge-to-edge instead of a lower-weighted strip, and shift the iPhone worldNode right a few pt so the crusher/skull is fully on-screen — it is the visual that motivates fleeing right. Mechanic-neutral.

(Optional, polish) Make updateCorridorPhysics() synchronous on gap change and animate only the decorative walls, eliminating the (currently unreachable) 0.5s body/visual desync window — a tidiness win, not a fairness fix.

verdictLine: Ship with one real fix — 1 NEW softlock (granted-but-banner-dismissed, no in-app recovery), 1 real iPad fairness/polish (door-1 + door-0 platform overlap), 1 real polish (static hint never names the GLITCHED sender), plus 1 first-encounter fairness speed-bump; 3 false-positives, hint-escalation already wired.

findings.title: Granted permission + banner dismissed without tapping = softlock (no in-app recovery)
findings.note: When notifications are GRANTED, the only unlock path is physically tapping the OS banner (NotificationDelegate.didReceive → InputEventBus.notificationTapped → unlockCurrentDoor). The scene handles only .notificationTapped and ignores .notificationReceived (fired on schedule, line 125 of NotificationManager), so no in-app tappable target is created. The in-app faux notification is created ONLY on permission-DENIED. If the player swipes/dismisses the foreground banner without tapping (very common), pendingNotificationId stays non-nil so requestNotification's `guard pendingNotificationId == nil` blocks re-requesting, AND the manager's pendingNotifications[id] is never cleared (only handleNotificationTapped/deactivate clear it) so observeForegroundForRearm's `!hasPendingNotification` re-arm never fires even after backgrounding. The give-up auto-unlock is unreachable because it needs a 3rd request the guard blocks. Net: that door is permanently soft-stuck. FIX: handle .notificationReceived(id:) for the genuine pending id by showing the same tappable in-app faux notification used on the denied path (always recoverable in-app), or clear the re-request guard once the notification has fired.

findings.title: iPad decoy fires 1s before genuine; intro never names the GLITCHED sender (first-encounter fairness)
findings.note: Decoy 'SYSTEM' alert is scheduled at max(0.5, realDelay-1.0)=2.0s, genuine 'GLITCHED' at 3.0s, so the first banner a first-timer sees is the decoy, and the instruction panel ('WAIT FOR THE ALERT') never tells them to tap the GLITCHED sender vs avoid SYSTEM. Mitigated: tapping the decoy is fully recoverable (handleDecoyTapped re-arms + narrator 'THAT WASN'T ME. TAP THE BELL AGAIN.'), and the give-up auto-unlock skips the decoy. Real but mild — a one-time speed-bump, not a softlock. FIX: brand the genuine sender in the intro ('look for GLITCHED, ignore SYSTEM') or delay the decoy until after the first successful unlock.

findings.title: iPad door geometry overlaps adjacent platforms (door 0 into spawn pad/rung-1; door 1 into top landing)
findings.note: Verified by geometry sim. Door 0 (center rungX(0)+100=230, w45) overlaps the 200-wide tier-0 pad's right edge by ~22.5pt and the tier-1 rung's left edge by ~27.5pt — the 'tangled spawn intersection' Claude flagged (note: the older 'L-shaped block' is gone in current code). Door 1 (center rungX(top)-70, w45) overlaps the 170-wide top-tier landing's left half by ~37.5pt, and on odd topTier its left edge also overlaps the previous rung by ~7.5pt. Both doors' 115pt ground-blockers sit embedded in solid platforms while LOCKED. Functionally the gate still works (blockers removed on unlock; reach budgets all within 85/130 — confirmed) and post-unlock the landing/exit are clean, so it is a visual-seam + potential mid-jump snag, not a softlock. iPad-only. FIX: nudge each door into the inter-platform gap so door art reads as distinct from the platforms.

findings.title: Static hintText never names the GLITCHED-vs-SYSTEM choice or the dropped rung
findings.note: Partial-correction of the reviewers: the hint system IS wired and DOES escalate — notePlayerStruggle (called on every death, line 1095) increments struggleCount and after >=2 struggles + >=8s triggers showDifficultyHintIfNeeded → hintText reveal (BaseLevelScene:744-768). What is true: the text is a single static sentence ('Wait for the notification, then tap it to unlock the door') that never adapts to the actual failure mode — a player repeatedly tapping the decoy or missing the signal-dropped rung gets the same unhelpful line. FIX (polish): branch hintText on a decoy-tap counter ('LOOK FOR THE GLITCHED SENDER, NOT SYSTEM').

findings.title: Faux-notification hit-tested with nodes(at:) breaks under iPad camera-follow
findings.note: SKScene.nodes(at: scenePoint) traverses the whole tree and accounts for each node's accumulated transform, INCLUDING nodes parented to the camera, so a camera-anchored uiLayer child is hit-tested correctly. This is the project-wide idiom for camera-anchored UI (Level28 AirDrop uses the same nodes(at:) for its camera-attached share button/keypad). The bell needs uiContains only because SKNode.contains() is parent-local; nodes(at:) does the conversion internally. Both paths work on iPad. Also: the faux notification only ever appears on permission-DENIED, where it remains tappable. Not a bug.

findings.title: uiLayer.addChild(instructionPanel!) force-unwrap could crash
findings.note: instructionPanel is assigned a fresh non-nil SKNode() two lines earlier (669-670) and force-unwrapped immediately after; it cannot be nil at line 672. No crash risk.

findings.title: drawFloorGrid floorY=140 ignores lifted iPad floor (grid floats below gameplay)
findings.note: Correct but trivial: the decorative grid (zPosition -15, alpha 0.3, scene-anchored) is hardcoded at y=140 while the iPad gameplay floor is at groundY≈126 and the climb spans far above. It is pure background decoration, behind everything, and on iPad it doesn't even camera-scroll. No gameplay or fairness impact. Lowest priority.

findings.title: Mechanic over-explained at t=0
findings.note: Design opinion, not a defect, and contradicted by the de-spoiling intent: the t=0 modal is deliberately abstract (doesn't reveal the leave-app/tap-alert trick) and the panel only names the bell affordance + 'wait for the alert' — it does NOT reveal the sender-choice puzzle, which stays for the player to deduce. Claude's review rates this exact balance a strength. Treating as no-action.

findings.title: setGrounded(false) delayed clear on didEnd — contact-state desync risk
findings.note: Checked the runtime-state-edge class the brief flagged. setGrounded uses a plain Bool flip (not a contact counter), and the 0.05s-delayed setGrounded(false) on didEnd is an intentional coyote-style buffer shared across levels — no counter to underflow, no animation/physics desync beyond the deliberate grace window. Not a bug.

topActions: Fix the GRANTED-but-banner-dismissed softlock: on .notificationReceived for the genuine pending id, present the same tappable in-app faux notification used on the denied path (or clear the re-request guard once the notification has fired) so a missed/swiped banner is always recoverable in-app.

Brand the genuine sender at first encounter: name 'GLITCHED' (and warn off 'SYSTEM') in the intro/instruction panel, or delay the decoy until after the first successful unlock, so the iPad decoy-before-genuine timing isn't an unfair first-time trap.
iPad polish: nudge door 0 out of the spawn-pad/rung-1 footprint and door 1 out of the top-landing footprint so each locked door reads as a distinct shape and can't snag Bit mid-jump on unlock.
Polish: branch hintText() on the failure mode (decoy-tap counter -> 'LOOK FOR THE GLITCHED SENDER, NOT SYSTEM') instead of one static sentence.

verdictLine: ship — 1 real polish (auto-solve poll contradicts the level's own user-initiated-paste claim), 1 real cosmetic (dead var), 4 false-positives, 2 already-fixed by the safety-net/de-spoil work

findings.title: Background clipboard poll auto-solves the puzzle and contradicts the level's 'string read is user-initiated only' claim
findings.note: On a real device usesHardware(.clipboard) is true, so DeviceManagerCoordinator.configure activates ClipboardManager's 0.5s polling timer, which reads UIPasteboard.general.string (guarded only by changeCount/hasStrings) and posts .clipboardUpdated -> handleGameInput -> checkPassword. So when the player returns with GLITCH3D copied, the level auto-unlocks BEFORE they tap PASTE, and the read is NOT user-initiated — directly contradicting the scene's repeated P1 comments (lines 106-118, 459-463, 578-590) and surfacing the iOS 'pasted from X' banner the comments claim to avoid. Codex was right that the .clipboardUpdated path is a non-explicit bypass; the deeper cause is the still-active poll, not just the event handler. Recommendation: for the clipboard level, gate the auto-poll off (or drop the .clipboardUpdated->checkPassword path here) so the visible PASTE control is the only solve path; keep the GameRootView:241 fallback button as the no-hardware route. Low player impact (puzzle still completes), so polish.

findings.title: clipboardScanLabel is dead storage — declared, never assigned or read
findings.note: private var clipboardScanLabel: SKLabelNode? is never written or read anywhere in the file. Harmless dead state; delete it. kimi correct.

findings.title: Literal answer GLITCH3D only appears in hintText() — first-timer can't know what to copy without the hint
findings.note: This is the intended de-spoil design, not a fairness gap. The terminal redacts the field (DE-SPOIL comment 441-443) and the two-line atmospheric tease ('IT REMEMBERS WHAT YOU LAST HELD' / 'THE KEY IS NOT HERE... SOMEWHERE YOU ALREADY WERE') plus 'EXTRACT & RETURN' point at the OS pasteboard mechanic. The escalating hint pipeline (notePlayerStruggle on death + the wall-clock no-progress safety-net) surfaces hintText() which names GLITCH3D. Seeding the literal string in-world as Claude suggests would re-spoil the puzzle. Intentional.

findings.title: Stuck-but-not-dying player never triggers hint escalation (struggle only increments on death)
findings.note: Addressed by the NO-PROGRESS HINT SAFETY-NET added to BaseLevelScene: lines 714-718 fire showDifficultyHintIfNeeded() once lastProgressAt (stamped at play start, line 490, and on progress) exceeds noProgressFallbackDelay (~22s), with NO dependence on notePlayerStruggle()/death. This is exactly the confusion-not-death failure mode both reviewers flagged. Both were reviewing the older build.

findings.title: Single spoiler hintText() returned on every call prevents graduated hints
findings.note: hintText() is intentionally the FINAL/most-concrete rung. Graduation is handled upstream by the shared hint pipeline (generic difficulty hint first via showDifficultyHint, then the level-specific hintText), not by varying this string per call. A struggle-indexed table inside the level would duplicate base-class behavior. Not a defect; the single concrete hint is by design as the last resort.

findings.title: iPad finale (terminal/PASTE/door/exit) off-screen to the right at t=0 — reads as 'staircase to nowhere'
findings.note: installCameraFollow snaps the camera to the low-left spawn, so the high-right finale is initially off-screen — but this is the intended horizontal-scrolling climb design (documented 220-260). The player ascends and the camera reveals the finale naturally, the standard platformer pattern; the camera-fixed 'EXTRACT & RETURN' HUD panel states the goal. Not a fairness or completability issue. At most a minor first-glance UX nitpick, not worth changing.

findings.title: doorBlocker! force-unwrap
findings.note: doorBlocker is assigned an SKNode() on the line immediately above the force-unwrapped use (392 then 396), so the unwrap can never be nil at that point. Local, provably-safe unwrap, not a crash risk. kimi flagged it as a rough edge but it is not a real hazard.

findings.title: isWideCanvas hard-codes 1000pt height instead of base-class helpers
findings.note: size.height > 1000 && size.width > designWidth is the dominant project convention — identical in L1, L10, L19, L20, L23, L28. Consistent with siblings; not a defect.

findings.title: iPhone & iPad jump-reach / locked-door trap within budget
findings.note: Confirmed not a bug — included for completeness. Door body 145 tall, center groundY+50 -> top groundY+122.5, ~16.5pt above Bit's ~91pt apex from platform top (groundY+15): intentionally un-jumpable until unlock() removes doorBlocker.physicsBody. iPad uses 118 center pitch (<130) and verticalTier rises clamped to 85. All within the 130/85 budget on both paths. Reviewers agreed; verified against current code.

topActions: Stop the clipboard auto-solve so the visible PASTE button is the real solve path: in the clipboard level, suppress the background poll's .clipboardUpdated->checkPassword auto-unlock (or skip the manager's string-read poll) so no non-user-initiated UIPasteboard.general.string read fires — this also honors the level's own P1 'paste from X' anti-banner claim. Keep the GameRootView fallback button intact.
Delete the dead clipboardScanLabel property (line 24).

verdictLine: ship — 0 crash/softlock, 2 real polish (illegible 0.3-alpha load-bearing stones; non-escalating + Control-Center-pointing hint), 1 stale doc (LEVEL-GUIDE download claim), and 3 false-positives/already-fixed (notePlayerStruggle present, isWideCanvas gate intentional, finale-offscreen by design)

findings.title: handleDeath() does not call notePlayerStruggle() — repeated falls don't escalate the hint
findings.note: Round-1 reviews were against the older build. CURRENT handleDeath() at line 911 calls notePlayerStruggle() before playBufferDeath(); base-class notePlayerStruggle (BaseLevelScene.swift:744) surfaces the difficulty hint at struggleCount>=2 && 8s. This is exactly the 'Review-driven fixes' escalation hook. No action.

findings.title: hintText() is a single static line and points to 'Control Center' which can mislead
findings.note: The struggle->hint pipeline now fires (already-fixed above), but hintText() returns one undifferentiated line 'Toggle WiFi in Control Center' for both the ON-to-cross-chasm and OFF-to-climb-wall beats, and names Control Center even though the in-scene mechanic is the real device WiFi/accessibility toggle. Real but low impact. Recommend: split via difficultyHintDidShow()/struggleCount-aware text (e.g. first 'Turn WiFi ON to build the bridge', later 'Drop the signal to climb past the wall').

findings.title: WiFi-ON stepping stones / OFF-step render at 0.3 alpha — load-bearing pieces read as decoration on first encounter
findings.note: Grounded: inactive wifiOn/wifiOff platforms are set to alpha 0.3 and the antenna/slash icons are scaled 0.5 (lines 495-501). The stones ARE the only bridge across the 232pt chasm, so a first-timer can't tell they're platforms vs the decorative signal art. Not a softlock (the mechanic still works once understood) but a genuine first-encounter legibility gap. Recommend ~0.5-0.6 alpha + a dashed/ghost fill for the inactive state and larger icons.

findings.title: Download/SIGNAL bar is cosmetic but LEVEL-GUIDE says completing the download unlocks the final section
findings.note: Code is internally honest: updateDownloadBar (666-668) comments it is purely cosmetic and handleExit (916) succeeds with no downloadCompleted gate; succeedLevel is idempotent. The in-game label only ever shows 'SIGNAL: STRONG/LOST' — it never tells the player to finish a download, so there's no in-game false objective. The mismatch is solely with LEVEL-GUIDE.md:138 ('Complete the download ... to unlock the final section'). Fix the doc (or rename the bar), not the level logic. Reviewers were right that the guide is stale; wrong that it misleads the player at runtime.

findings.title: isWideCanvas gate (height>1000 && width>430) is brittle / mis-handles split-view/compact iPad
findings.note: The gate deliberately matches the base-class helpers' own size.height>1000 threshold (playableGroundY/playableCeilingY/verticalTier/fillTierCount all guard on size.height>1000). A split-view/compact-height iPad falls through to buildPhoneLevel — a complete, centered, self-contained, fully-completable course (scale clamps at 1.0, course never overflows). That is the intended graceful fallback, not a softlock. Not a bug.

findings.title: On iPad the finale trap + stones sit off-screen right until the camera scrolls (no preview)
findings.note: This is by design: the composed iPad course is intentionally ~1.5-3x viewport width and uses installCameraFollow (line 776). Staging the signature trap as a HIGH finale the player climbs toward is the deliberate paced-beat structure (comment 280-299). A preview would undercut the reveal. Cosmetic-at-most, not worth changing.

findings.title: Stale didEnd delayed setGrounded(false) can briefly false-unground when walking between adjacent stones
findings.note: Hunted the runtime/state-edge class. didEnd schedules an unconditional 0.05s setGrounded(false); if a new didBegin (next stone) re-grounds in that window, the stale closure still fires false. BUT isGrounded is a plain bool (not a contact counter, so no underflow), PlayerController re-sets coyoteTimer every grounded frame and has a jump buffer (PlayerController.swift:74-89,112), and clearGroundedIfStandingOn handles the real de-solidify case immediately. Net player-visible impact is negligible and the pattern is shared base infra, not L13-specific. Logged for completeness only; not worth a fix here.

topActions: Render the load-bearing WiFi-ON stones and the WiFi-OFF step at ~0.5-0.6 alpha (not 0.3) with a dashed/ghost fill and larger icons so first-time players read them as platforms, not decoration (Level13_WiFi.swift:516-517,495-501)
Make hintText escalating/beat-aware and stop pointing at 'Control Center' (Level13_WiFi.swift:927-929) — e.g. first 'Turn WiFi ON to build the bridge', then 'Drop the signal to climb past the wall'
Update LEVEL-GUIDE.md:138 to remove the 'complete the download to unlock the final section' claim (the bar is intentionally cosmetic), or rename the bar to a pure signal-strength readout

verdictLine: ship — 0 crash/softlock survivors; the headline iPad-void and finale-lane fears are already-fixed/false-positive against current code; only real survivors are 2 polish items (max-budget diagonal climb, fallback-button spoiler).
